title: Women in Engineering: Progress and Persistent Barriers
subtitle: 美国工程教育的现状与挑战

paragraphs.en: Engineering in the U.S. has long been a male-dominated profession. Fifty years ago, it looked like that might change. In 1970, the percentage of women majoring in engineering was less than 1%. In 1979, that number was 9%. Many hoped women would continue to enter the field at the same rate.
paragraphs.zh: 在美国，工程学长期以来一直是男性主导的职业。五十年前，情况似乎有望改变。1970年，工程学专业的女性比例不到1%。到1979年，这一数字达到9%。许多人希望女性以同样的速度继续进入该领域。

paragraphs.en: But that's not what happened. Today, only 21% of engineering majors are women, a number largely unchanged since 2000. I am a historian who, along with my colleagues, surveyed 251 women engineers who graduated from college in the 1970s. These pioneers reflected on the challenges they faced — and had advice for women entering the field today.
paragraphs.zh: 但事实并非如此。如今，仅21%的工程专业学生是女性，这一数字自2000年以来基本没有变化。我是一名历史学家，与同事一起调查了251位于20世纪70年代大学毕业的女工程师。这些先驱者反思了她们曾面临的挑战，并为当今进入该领域的女性提供了建议。

paragraphs.en: One survey taker explained, 'The greatest challenge for me was continuing to believe in myself, when all the messages I was getting were that I would never be taken seriously or promoted or given raises at the same rate as men, who were clearly less qualified and not as smart as I was.' A chemical engineer who worked in manufacturing agreed, 'You have to prove yourself just because you are female. And you have to work twice as hard!' A civil engineer said, 'We are women engineers. People don't refer to a man as a man engineer — he's an engineer. We are constantly reminded that we don't truly belong.'
paragraphs.zh: 一位受访者解释说：“对我而言最大的挑战是继续相信自己，因为我得到的所有信息都是，我永远不会像男性那样被认真对待、获得晋升或加薪，而他们显然不如我有资格，也不如我聪明。”一位在制造业工作的化学工程师表示赞同：“就因为你是女性，你就得证明自己。而且你必须加倍努力！”一位土木工程师说：“我们是‘女工程师’。人们不会称男性为‘男工程师’——他就是工程师。我们不断被提醒，我们并不真正属于这里。”

paragraphs.en: Another civil engineer stated, 'On many levels, you're never quite one of the group.' Women also talked about family caregiving responsibilities. A retired vice president from a major chemical company stated, 'Young women engineers are on an equal footing until they have children, then they struggle to balance work and family — and compete with men who don't have the same household responsibilities.' But over the years things have changed a lot. Young women engineers are more accepted mostly because there are just more of them.
paragraphs.zh: 另一位土木工程师表示：“在很多层面上，你永远都不完全是团队中的一员。”女性也谈到了家庭照护责任。一家大型化工公司的退休副总裁说：“年轻女工程师在生育前处于平等地位，之后便难以平衡工作与家庭，并要与没有同等家庭责任的男性竞争。”但多年来情况已发生很大变化。年轻女工程师更容易被接受，主要因为她们的人数更多了。

paragraphs.en: Many women engineers hailed the benefits of their chosen career. A program manager in manufacturing stated that engineering is the best degree. A mechanical engineer said, 'It will give you the flexibility to do almost anything. It is also satisfying to see the effects of what you have done.'
paragraphs.zh: 许多女工程师盛赞她们所选职业的益处。一位制造业项目经理称工程学是最好的学位。一位机械工程师说：“它能赋予你灵活性去做几乎任何事。看到自己所做之事产生的影响也令人满足。”
